paragraphs.summary: This supervisory statement is addressed to all insurance firms within the scope of Solvency II reporting under UK generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) rather than using international accoun

The statement was subject to public consultation,1 and reflects feedback received by the PRA. Some comments suggested altering wording and these suggestions have been accepted where clarity would be i

The PRA is publishing this statement to expand on its general approach as set out in its Insurance Approach document.2 By clearly and consistently explaining its views in this area, the PRA seeks to a
The PRA has considered matters to which it is required to have regard, and it considers that this statement is compatible with the Regulatory Principles and relevant provisions of the Legislative and
Rule 5.4 in the Valuation Part of the PRA Rulebook contains a derogation (‘the derogation’) for firms within the scope of Solvency II for which

The PRA expects that where UK GAAP and IFRS are consistent, in that they apply the same requirements as regards recognition and valuation, the derogation will not apply. In such cases firms will not i

The PRA expects any firm wishing to take advantage of the derogation to provide supporting evidence regarding the second and third conditions set out in paragraph [1.5](/guidance/supervisory-statement
The derogation relates to Valuation 5.1 and 5.2, but does not affect the application o

Any firm that relies on the derogation will still be expected to apply in full the remaining valuation requirements of the PRA Rulebook, regardless of whether the UK GAAP provisions are consistent wit

For insurance firms, most of the differences between UK GAAP and international accounting standards relate only to the level of detail which must be disclosed. Since the derogation addresses recogniti
